caption.full:
Mark H. Rapaport's 'Godhead' premiered at Fantasia Festival

Mark H. Rapaport's tense, black-and-white chamber drama 'Godhead' premiered at Montreal's Fantasia Festival. The film blends religious fanaticism with psychological horror, inspired by the monochrome style of 'The Lighthouse'. Actor Al Warren's suggestion led to a much more horrifying ending.

Does the film’s voyeuristic black-and-white style deepen the tension or feel like a gimmick?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #FantasiaFestival #Godhead #MarkHRapaport
caption.firstComment: The twins’ gun-toting prophecy lands as bold social critique, not edgy shock—anyone who thinks otherwise is defending respectability over truth in horror.

facebook.text: Mark H. Rapaport's new thriller 'Godhead' premiered at Fantasia Festival, featuring a priest and armed twins. The film's ending was reshaped into something horrifying by an actor's suggestion. What did you think of the film's tense, black-and-white style? Let us know in the comments!

youtube.title: Mark H. Rapaport's 'Godhead' Has Horrifying Ending
youtube.description:
Mark H. Rapaport's film 'Godhead' premiered at Montreal's Fantasia Festival. The black-and-white chamber drama blends religious fanaticism with psychological horror. Actor Al Warren suggested a change that led to a much more horrifying ending. The film stars Al Warren, Sarah Coffey, and Kimball Farley.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.
